Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer

Class FlixelTimer

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer.FlixelTimer
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelUpdatable
public final class FlixelTimer extends Object implements FlixelUpdatable, FlixelDestroyable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable
Frame-based countdown timer.

Advance time by calling FlixelTimerManager.update(float) once per frame (done automatically for getGlobalManager()). The elapsed argument should already include Flixel.getTimeScale().

Prefer FlixelTimerManager.start(float, FlixelTimerListener, int), wait(float, FlixelTimerListener), or loop(float, FlixelTimerListener, int) so pooled instances are reused.

The callback runs on the next update (one frame later), loops is ignored.

The timer is removed from its manager before the final callback runs.

  • Field Details

    • active

      public boolean active
      Whether the timer is currently active.

    • finished

      public boolean finished
      Whether the timer has finished.

    • time

      public float time
      The time to complete the timer.

    • loops

      public int loops
      Requested loop count. 0 means infinite (only when time is greater than zero).

    • elapsedLoops

      public int elapsedLoops
      The number of loops the timer has completed.

    • elapsedTime

      public float elapsedTime
      The time elapsed since the timer started.

      @Nullable public @Nullable FlixelTimerListener onComplete
      The callback to run when the timer completes.

    • start

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelTimer start(float timeSeconds, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelTimerListener onComplete, int loopCount)
      Restarts this timer on its current manager without allocating a new instance.
      Parameters:
      timeSeconds - The time to complete the timer.
      onComplete - The callback to run when the timer completes.
      loopCount - The number of loops to run the timer.
      Returns:
      The timer.

    • loop

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelTimer loop(float timeSeconds, @NotNull @NotNull FlixelTimerListener onEachLoop, int loopCount)
      Starts a pooled timer registered with the global manager.
      Parameters:
      timeSeconds - The time to complete the timer.
      onEachLoop - The callback to run when the timer completes each loop.
      loopCount - The number of loops to run the timer.
      Returns:
      The timer.
